one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding received from Indigenous Services Canada for public health measures, income assistance initiatives, and costs related to reopening
economic_notes: Frog Lake First Nation has numerous business enterprises including energy resources, oilfield services, off-reserve housing, travel centre, economic development, gravel and concrete, ranching, and construction operations. COVID-19 caused decline in FNDF revenues and business revenues. Four minority partnership investments also held (Seven Lakes Oilfield Services LP, Pimee Well Services LP, Pimee Well Services Ltd., IMI Brokerage LP).
notes: Audit opinion is QUALIFIED due to inability to obtain sufficient audit evidence on investments in government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method. 15 entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). 4 minority partnership investments also held. Restricted cash includes Province of Alberta externally restricted funds ($54,377), and internally restricted legal/housing ($9,177) and capital projects ($2,421,239). Federal Trust Funds of $3,262,200 held separately by Government of Canada. CMHC replacement reserve unfunded ($281,574). Significant COVID-19 additional ISC funding of $7,885,304 received. Investment loss of $4,186,144 recorded due to equity losses in GBEs (Frog Lake Energy Resources operations declined significantly). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $36,040,714 + ISETS $771,379 + CMHC $212,394 = $37,024,487. Morning Sky Health and Wellness Society revenue ($4,614,579) treated as OSR as it appears to be a related entity transfer rather than a direct government transfer.
